schema_context:
Table: Best Artworks of All Time
Rows: 50
Columns: 8

FIELD INFORMATION:
Field: id
  - Data Type: int64
  - Inferred Type: number
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents a unique identifier assigned to each artwork in the dataset, enabling easy reference and distinction among the 50 listed "Best Artworks of All Time."
  - Value Range: 0.0 to 49.0
  - Mean: 24.50

Field: name
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the names of renowned artists whose works are featured in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset, identifying the creator of each artwork.

Field: years
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the time span (years) during which each listed artwork was created or completed, indicating the period of artistic production for masterpieces in the art history domain.

Field: genre
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 31
  - Semantic Meaning: The "genre" field categorizes each artwork by its artistic style or movement, often listing one or more relevant genres to describe the artwork's aesthetic or historical context.

Field: nationality
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 17
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the country or countries associated with an artist's origin or cultural background, indicating the national identity relevant to artworks featured in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset.

Field: bio
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field contains biographical summaries of notable artists, including their names, birth and death dates, nationalities, and pronunciation or transliterations, providing contextual background for their artworks in the art history domain.

Field: wikipedia
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field contains Wikipedia URLs for artists, providing reference links to biographical information about each artist featured among the best artworks of all time.

Field: paintings
  - Data Type: int64
  - Inferred Type: number
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 48
  - Semantic Meaning: This field likely represents the total number of paintings created or attributed to each artist in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset.
  - Value Range: 24.0 to 877.0
  - Mean: 168.92

DATAFRAME COLUMN NAMES:
The DataFrame will have these EXACT column names that you MUST use for indexing:
  1. "id"
  2. "name"
  3. "years"
  4. "genre"
  5. "nationality"
  6. "bio"
  7. "wikipedia"
  8. "paintings"

CRITICAL: Use ONLY these exact column names when accessing DataFrame columns:
Examples: df['id'], df['name']

table_metadata.formatted_summary:
## Table 1: Best_Artworks_of_All_Time (50 rows × 8 columns)

### Description
Data loaded from CSV file: 20.csv (Dataset: Best Artworks of All Time)

### Schema (8 fields)
  - id -- type: int64, values: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, ..., 45, 46, 47, 48, 49
  - name -- type: object, values: Albrecht Dürer, Alfred Sisley, Amedeo Modigliani, Andrei Rublev, Andy Warhol, ..., Sandro Botticelli, Titian, Vasiliy Kandinskiy, Vincent van Gogh, William Turner
  - years -- type: object, values: 1266 - 1337, 1360 - 1430, 1395 - 1441, 1445 - 1510, 1450 - 1516, ..., 1898 - 1967, 1904 - 1989, 1907 - 1954, 1912 – 1956, 1928 – 1987
  - genre -- type: object, values: Abstract Expressionism, Baroque, Byzantine Art, Cubism, Early Renaissance, ..., "Surrealism,Impressionism", Symbolism, "Symbolism,Art Nouveau", "Symbolism,Expressionism", "Symbolism,Post-Impressionism"
  - nationality -- type: object, values: American, Austrian, Belgian, British, Dutch, ..., Mexican, Norwegian, Russian, Spanish, "Spanish,Greek"
  - bio -- type: object, values: Albrecht Dürer (; German: [ˈʔalbʁɛçt ˈdyːʁɐ]; 21 May 1471 – 6 April 1528) sometimes spelt in English..., "Alessandro (c. 1445 – May 17, 1510), known as Sandro Botticelli (Italian: [ˈsandro bottiˈtʃɛlli]), w...", Alfred Sisley (; French: [sislɛ]; 30 October 1839 – 29 January 1899) was an Impressionist landscape ..., Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January..., "Andrei Rublev (Russian: Андре́й Рублёв, IPA: [ɐnˈdrʲej rʊˈblʲɵf], also transliterated as Andrey Rubl...", ..., Sir Peter Paul Rubens (; Dutch: [ˈrybə(n)s]; 28 June 1577 – 30 May 1640) was a Flemish artist. He is..., Tiziano Vecelli or Tiziano Vecellio (pronounced [titˈtsjaːno veˈtʃɛlljo]; c. 1488/1490 – 27 August 1..., Vincent Willem van Gogh (Dutch: [ˈvɪnsɛnt ˈʋɪləm vɑŋ ˈɣɔx] (listen); 30 March 1853 – 29 July 1890) w..., "Wassily Wassilyevich Kandinsky (Russian: Васи́лий Васи́льевич Канди́нский, tr. Vasíliy Vasílʹevich K...", Édouard Manet (US: ; UK: ; French: [edwaʁ manɛ]; 23 January 1832 – 30 April 1883) was a French moder...
  - wikipedia -- type: object, values: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albrecht_Dürer, http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alfred_Sisley, http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani, http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrei_Rublev, http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caravaggio, ..., https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michelangelo, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Cézanne, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Klee, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piet_Mondrian, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raphael
  - paintings -- type: int64, values: 24, 31, 43, 47, 49, ..., 328, 336, 439, 702, 877

### Sample Data (first 5 rows)
```
   id                name        years                         genre nationality                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       bio                                       wikipedia  paintings
0   0   Amedeo Modigliani  1884 - 1920                 Expressionism     Italian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January 1920) was an Italian Jewish painter and sculptor who worked mainly in France. He is known for portraits and nudes in a modern style characterized by elongation of faces, necks, and figures that were not received well during his lifetime but later found acceptance. Modigliani spent his youth in Italy, where he studied the art of antiquity and the Renaissance. In 1906 he moved to Paris, where he came into contact with such artists as Pablo Picasso and Constantin Brâncuși. By 1912 Modigliani was exhibiting highly stylized sculptures with Cubists of the Section d'Or group at the Salon d'Automne.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani        193
1   1  Vasiliy Kandinskiy  1866 - 1944  Expressionism,Abstractionism     Russian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Wassily Wassilyevich Kandinsky (Russian: Васи́лий Васи́льевич Канди́нский, tr. Vasíliy Vasílʹevich Kandínskiy) (16 December [O.S. 4 December] 1866 – 13 December 1944) was a Russian painter and art theorist.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wassily_Kandinsky         88
2   2        Diego Rivera  1886 - 1957       Social Realism,Muralism     Mexican                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez, known as Diego Rivera (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈdjeɣo riˈβeɾa]; December 8, 1886 – November 24, 1957) was a prominent Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the Mexican mural movement in Mexican art. Between 1922 and 1953, Rivera painted murals in, among other places, Mexico City, Chapingo, Cuernavaca, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York City. In 1931, a retrospective exhibition of his works was held at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Rivera had a volatile marriage with fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Rivera         70
3   3        Claude Monet  1840 - 1926                 Impressionism      French  Oscar-Claude Monet (; French: [klod mɔnɛ]; 14 November 1840 – 5 December 1926) was a French painter, a founder of French Impressionist painting and the most consistent and prolific practitioner of the movement's philosophy of expressing one's perceptions before nature, especially as applied to plein air landscape painting. The term "Impressionism" is derived from the title of his painting Impression, soleil levant (Impression, Sunrise), which was exhibited in 1874 in the first of the independent exhibitions mounted by Monet and his associates as an alternative to the Salon de Paris.Monet's ambition of documenting the French countryside led him to adopt a method of painting the same scene many times in order to capture the changing of light and the passing of the seasons. From 1883, Monet lived in Giverny, where he purchased a house and property and began a vast landscaping project which included lily ponds that would become the subjects of his best-known works. In 1899, he began painting the water lilies, first in vertical views with a Japanese bridge as a central feature and later in the series of large-scale paintings that was to occupy him continuously for the next 20 years of his life.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Monet         73
4   4       Rene Magritte  1898 - 1967      Surrealism,Impressionism     Belgian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                René François Ghislain Magritte (French: [ʁəne fʁɑ̃swa ɡilɛ̃ maɡʁit]; 21 November 1898 – 15 August 1967) was a Belgian Surrealist artist. He became well known for creating a number of witty and thought-provoking images. Often depicting ordinary objects in an unusual context, his work is known for challenging observers' preconditioned perceptions of reality. His imagery has influenced Pop art, minimalist and conceptual art.      http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/René_Magritte        194
```

questions.question: Which artists have both a high number of paintings and biographies that emphasize innovation or major influence on artistic movements?

analysis.design_rationale: A bar chart lets us rank artists by the number of paintings and directly compare their output. Highlighting bars where biographies mention 'innovation' or 'influence on movements' draws immediate attention to those artists that intersect both the quantitative and qualitative criteria. This chart type is compact and readable for the dataset size (50 rows), and by sorting bars and overlaying annotation, it spotlights the artists who meet both criteria.
analysis.target_insight: Shows which artists have the highest painting counts and whose biographies emphasize innovation or major influence, with direct visual marking of those who satisfy both criteria.

chart_encodings.color: Bar color: highlighted if biography mentions 'innovation' or 'influence'
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Sort bars descending by number of paintings; annotate or differently color bars where criteria are met; add count labels; show legend for highlighted artists

transformations: Filter df['bio'] for biographies containing keywords 'innovation', 'innovative', 'influence', or 'movement'
Create boolean mask 'innovator' for artists where df['bio'] contains relevant keywords
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
df['innovator'] = df['bio'].str.contains('innovation|innovative|influence|movement', case=False, regex=True)
df_plot = df.sort_values('paintings', ascending=False)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 9))
bar_colors = np.where(df_plot['innovator'], 'tab:red', 'tab:blue')
bars = ax.barh(df_plot['name'], df_plot['paintings'], color=bar_colors, edgecolor='black')
for i, (val, innov) in enumerate(zip(df_plot['paintings'], df_plot['innovator'])):
    ax.text(val + 5, i, str(int(val)), va='center', color='black', fontsize=10)
ax.set_xlabel('paintings')
ax.set_ylabel('name')
ax.set_title('Artists with High painting counts and Biographies emphasizing Innovation or Influence', fontsize=14)
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor='tab:red', edgecolor='black', label='Bio emphasizes innovation/influence'),Patch(facecolor='tab:blue', edgecolor='black', label='Other artists')]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='lower right')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

analysis.design_rationale: This approach uses a scatterplot where each artist is positioned by the number of paintings, and bubble size encodes the frequency of bio keywords related to innovation/influence. The plot allows visibility of both quantity (paintings) and intensity of biographical emphasis (keyword counts), giving nuanced insight into which artists are most associated with innovation/influence and productive output. Annotating top bubbles adds narrative context.
analysis.target_insight: Maps artists by painting output and bio keyword frequency, visually revealing those with the strongest combo of prolific output and innovation/influence mentions.

chart_encodings.size: Bubble size: frequency of relevant keywords detected in bio
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Annotations for top bubbles; custom colormap for nationality for additional context

transformations: Count occurrences of 'innovation', 'innovative', 'influence', and 'movement' (any case) in df['bio'] for each artist and store in 'keyword_count'
Filter artists with keyword_count > 0 and painting counts above mean (168.92) to focus the scatterplot
Map 'nationality' to discrete colors using colormap for additional exploration
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
keyword_pattern = r'(innovation|innovative|influence|movement)'
df['keyword_count'] = df['bio'].str.count(keyword_pattern, flags=np.__dict__['IGNORECASE'])
df_plot = df[(df['keyword_count'] > 0) & (df['paintings'] > 168.92)].copy()
nationalities = df_plot['nationality'].unique()
nationality_map = dict(zip(nationalities, np.arange(len(nationalities))))
color_idxs = df_plot['nationality'].map(nationality_map)
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis'].resampled(len(nationalities))
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 7))
sc = ax.scatter(df_plot['paintings'], df_plot['keyword_count'], s=df_plot['keyword_count']*80 + 100, c=color_idxs, cmap=cmap, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.8)
for i, row in df_plot.iterrows():
    if row['keyword_count'] > df_plot['keyword_count'].max() - 1:
        label = row['name'] + '\n(' + str(int(row['paintings'])) + ' paintings, keywords: ' + str(row['keyword_count']) + ')'
        ax.text(row['paintings'] + 10, row['keyword_count'], label, fontsize=10, color='black')
ax.set_xlabel('paintings', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Bio keyword count', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Which artists have BOTH high painting counts AND bios stressing innovation/influence?', fontsize=14)
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
legend_elements = [Line2D([0],[0], marker='o', color='w', markerfacecolor=cmap(idx), label=nat, markersize=14, markeredgecolor='black') for nat, idx in nationality_map.items()]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, title='nationality', bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

0   0   Amedeo Modigliani  1884 - 1920                 Expressionism     Italian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January 1920) was an Italian Jewish painter and sculptor who worked mainly in France. He is known for portraits and nudes in a modern style characterized by elongation of faces, necks, and figures that were not received well during his lifetime but later found acceptance. Modigliani spent his youth in Italy, where he studied the art of antiquity and the Renaissance. In 1906 he moved to Paris, where he came into contact with such artists as Pablo Picasso and Constantin Brâncuși. By 1912 Modigliani was exhibiting highly stylized sculptures with Cubists of the Section d'Or group at the Salon d'Automne.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani        193

2   2        Diego Rivera  1886 - 1957       Social Realism,Muralism     Mexican                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez, known as Diego Rivera (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈdjeɣo riˈβeɾa]; December 8, 1886 – November 24, 1957) was a prominent Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the Mexican mural movement in Mexican art. Between 1922 and 1953, Rivera painted murals in, among other places, Mexico City, Chapingo, Cuernavaca, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York City. In 1931, a retrospective exhibition of his works was held at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Rivera had a volatile marriage with fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Rivera         70
3   3        Claude Monet  1840 - 1926                 Impressionism      French  Oscar-Claude Monet (; French: [klod mɔnɛ]; 14 November 1840 – 5 December 1926) was a French painter, a founder of French Impressionist painting and the most consistent and prolific practitioner of the movement's philosophy of expressing one's perceptions before nature, especially as applied to plein air landscape painting. The term "Impressionism" is derived from the title of his painting Impression, soleil levant (Impression, Sunrise), which was exhibited in 1874 in the first of the independent exhibitions mounted by Monet and his associates as an alternative to the Salon de Paris.Monet's ambition of documenting the French countryside led him to adopt a method of painting the same scene many times in order to capture the changing of light and the passing of the seasons. From 1883, Monet lived in Giverny, where he purchased a house and property and began a vast landscaping project which included lily ponds that would become the subjects of his best-known works. In 1899, he began painting the water lilies, first in vertical views with a Japanese bridge as a central feature and later in the series of large-scale paintings that was to occupy him continuously for the next 20 years of his life.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Monet         73
4   4       Rene Magritte  1898 - 1967      Surrealism,Impressionism     Belgian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                René François Ghislain Magritte (French: [ʁəne fʁɑ̃swa ɡilɛ̃ maɡʁit]; 21 November 1898 – 15 August 1967) was a Belgian Surrealist artist. He became well known for creating a number of witty and thought-provoking images. Often depicting ordinary objects in an unusual context, his work is known for challenging observers' preconditioned perceptions of reality. His imagery has influenced Pop art, minimalist and conceptual art.      http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/René_Magritte        194
